variables.name: xyz_actionable_insights
variables.meaning: actionable insights or reusable findings to route
variables.example: prompt-kit registry extraction and selector logic

variables.name: xyz_app_context
variables.meaning: current application context, repository state, and operator constraints

variables.name: xyz_available_evidence
variables.meaning: available docs, screenshots, repositories, logs, or runtime proof

variables.name: xyz_branch_pr_sprint
variables.meaning: known branch, pull request, or sprint identifier

variables.name: xyz_candidate_repositories_or_org
variables.meaning: candidate repositories or organization to prioritize

variables.name: xyz_constraints
variables.meaning: material safety, time, tooling, platform, or scope constraints
variables.example: no secrets; Windows-first; no workbook binaries in Git

variables.name: xyz_context_path
variables.meaning: path to a context, plan, or handoff artifact

variables.name: xyz_current_circumstances
variables.meaning: current blockers, urgency, available tools, and operator circumstances
variables.example: limited provider quota; clean main; overnight run desired

variables.name: xyz_device_os_access
variables.meaning: devices, operating systems, and access available to the operator
variables.example: Windows 11 workstation with PowerShell 7 and WSL Ubuntu

variables.name: xyz_experience_level
variables.meaning: operator experience level for the tutorial
variables.example: intermediate IT project manager and repository operator

variables.name: xyz_local_path
variables.meaning: verified local application or repository path

variables.name: xyz_runtime_sprint
variables.meaning: runtime-proof sprint name and exact behavior under test

variables.name: xyz_source_material
variables.meaning: source prompts, notes, handoffs, or requirements to consolidate
variables.example: P01, P07, and current sprint handoff

variables.name: xyz_target_artifact
variables.meaning: actual artifact or deliverable that must be produced
variables.example: versioned prompt-registry JSON and selector CLI

variables.name: xyz_user_goal
variables.meaning: concrete outcome the user wants from the application
variables.example: select and render the correct prompt without manually browsing the workbook
